{"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.92,"matched_text":"The ideal tire pressure for your road bike depends on several factors, including:

1. **Tire type**: Road bike tires come in various widths, materials, and tread patterns. Different tires have different pressure recommendations.
2. **Rider weight**: Your weight, as well as the weight of any luggage or accessories, affects the ideal pressure.
3. **Riding style**: If you're a more aggressive rider, you may prefer a slightly higher pressure for better handling and responsiveness.
4. **Road surface**: Riding on rough roads or cobblestones may require a lower pressure for comfort and traction, while smooth roads can handle higher pressures.","distilled_fact":"The ideal tire pressure for a road bike depends on tire type, rider weight, riding style, and road surface."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.9,"matched_text":"The recommended pressure range is usually printed on the sidewall of your tires.","distilled_fact":"The recommended pressure range is usually printed on the sidewall of a tire."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.88,"matched_text":"A common starting point for road bike tires is:
	* 90-100 PSI (6.2-6.9 bar) for narrower tires (23-25mm)
	* 80-90 PSI (5.5-6.2 bar) for wider tires (25-28mm)","distilled_fact":"A common starting point for road bike tires is 90-100 PSI (6.2-6.9 bar) for narrower tires (23-25mm) and 80-90 PSI (5.5-6.2 bar) for wider tires (25-28mm)."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.86,"matched_text":"Use a pressure gauge: Invest in a good-quality pressure gauge, as the one on your pump might not be accurate.","distilled_fact":"A good-quality pressure gauge may be more accurate than the gauge on a pump."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.87,"matched_text":"Check pressure when cold: Measure pressure when the tires are cold, as heat generated by riding can affect the reading.","distilled_fact":"Tire pressure should be measured when the tires are cold because riding heat can affect the reading."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.84,"matched_text":"Check both tires: Ensure both tires are at the same pressure to maintain even handling and prevent uneven wear.","distilled_fact":"Both tires should be at the same pressure to maintain even handling and prevent uneven wear."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.83,"matched_text":"Check pressure regularly: Tire pressure can drop over time due to natural leakage or temperature changes.","distilled_fact":"Tire pressure can drop over time due to natural leakage or temperature changes."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"fact","confidence":0.81,"matched_text":"Carry a pump or CO2 cartridges: Be prepared for a flat tire by carrying a pump or CO2 cartridges on your ride.","distilled_fact":"A rider should carry a pump or CO2 cartridges to be prepared for a flat tire."}]}
